theory.canonical_statement: a\sqrt{\rho}}\right)^2 \end{equation} For pure states $\rho = \ket{\psi}\bra{\psi}$ and $\sigma = \ket{\phi}\bra{\phi}$, this reduces to $F_{\text{quantum}} = |\braket{\psi|\phi}|^2$, the squared overlap between state vectors. \subsection{Bell Inequality Violation and Entanglement Verification} Entangled holographic projections maintain quantum correlations that can be verified through violation of Bell inequalities. The Clauser-Horne-Shimony-Holt inequality provides the standard test: \begin{equation} |S_{\text{CHSH}}| = |E(a, b) - E(a, b') + E(a', b) + E(a', b')| \leq 2 \end{equation} where $E(a, b) = \braket{A_a \otimes B_b}$ denotes the expectation value of the product of measurements with settings $a$ and $b$ on the two subsystems. Classical local hidden variable theories cannot exceed the bound of 2, while quantum mechanics permits values up to $2\sqrt{2} \approx 2.828$ (the Tsirelson bound). For maximally entangled Bell states propagating through the rotating helix system, the CHSH parameter evolves as: \begin{equation} S_{\text{CHSH}}(t, \Omega) = 2\sqrt{2} \exp[-\Gamma_{\text{dec}}(\Omega) t] |\cos[\Delta\Phi(
theory.technical_interpretation: Definition of E(a, B) is an algebraic definition, written E(a, b) = braketA_a ⊗ B_b. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from A_a, B_b, defining E(a, b). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
